Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2025-09-18 6:15 p.m.

6:16 p.m.
I think there's some issues with the g2
ninkosan 2025-09-19 1:13 p.m.

Steve ● 7acc 2025-09-19 2:11 p.m.
I thought the G2 topped out at about 18db?
2:13 p.m.
15db is recommended according to https://wiki.uniteng.com/en/meshtastic/station-g2
Station G2
The Station G2 features a 35dBm high power PA, specially designed ultra-low noise figure LNA and a Fast-Transient DC-DC. The Station Series aims to provide the best RF performance in all series with credit card size and rugged construction.
ninkosan 2025-09-19 2:19 p.m.
Yeh I’m set to 15 at the moment for testing purposes at least
ninkosan 2025-09-20 1:28 p.m.
Update: G2 is definitely perma-deaf in current location. Wondering if it’s just too noisy there for it (noise floor is… bad). have ordered a filter to test with and see if that makes any difference

Flood Map: Water Level Elevation Map
Flood Map shows the map of the area which could get flooded if the water level rises to a particular elevation. It may help flood risk assessment or flood management. Sea level rise map. Bathymetric map, ocean depth.

Tyler - OlneyLoftBase

Buzzard 2025-10-04 6:21 a.m.

Buzzard 2025-10-04 6:32 a.m.
@Tyler - OlneyLoftBase I have a 90% complete repeater node I was going to use for meshtastic - but now maybe worth sticking it north of Welly to see if we can get coverage north and west of me.

@Tyler - OlneyLoftBase I have a 90% complete repeater node I was going to use for meshtastic - but now maybe worth sticking it north of Welly to see if we can get coverage north and west of me.

Buzzard 2025-10-05 7:44 a.m.

Buzzard 2025-10-05 7:55 a.m.

ShadowDemon 2025-10-08 4:58 p.m.
let me know if you find a repeater spot, i would be willing to donate for the cause. easiest way is to find a goods spot with your client and see if you can log into home and one of the ohters in the area, wednesfield, wolverhampton, sedgley, sandwell, wv11 or wv11 yagi. then you will make it onto the mesh. we are working all the way down to summerset and south wales (edited)
justanotherhandlefor 2025-10-08 5:01 p.m.
Well thank you new friend I will keep testing. Shame the bluetooth is quite so pathetic on the Heltec otherwise I could connect around my house more easily
ShadowDemon 2025-10-08 5:02 p.m.
remove the spring and replace with a 3cm straight wire. A bit strange though my v3.2 is in the upper far corner of the house and works well (edited)
justanotherhandlefor 2025-10-08 5:04 p.m.
Well I'm two rooms over from my office where the client is & no joy. The wire is a good tip
ShadowDemon 2025-10-08 5:04 p.m.

likkleidiot_nwo 2025-11-09 8:58 a.m.
Who owns Clee Hill East.. I own Crows Nest 5 that sometimes bridges to Clee Hill East, I'm deploying a crows Nest 6 with hopes it Bridges the gap..just wanted to know if it's been moved at all recently just so I'm not whistling in the wind literally..lol
likkleidiot_nwo
Who owns Clee Hill East.. I own Crows Nest 5 that sometimes bridges to Clee Hill East, I'm deploying a crows Nest 6 with hopes it Bridges the gap..just wanted to know if it's been moved at all recently just so I'm not whistling in the wind literally..lol
Steve ● 7acc 2025-11-09 9:08 a.m.
@rugerfanuk should be able to answer that one
likkleidiot_nwo 2025-11-09 9:10 a.m.
Looks like there is another repeater bringing in the link from Clee Hill East to Manchester now .it's currently unknown on my list 5b
likkleidiot_nwo
Who owns Clee Hill East.. I own Crows Nest 5 that sometimes bridges to Clee Hill East, I'm deploying a crows Nest 6 with hopes it Bridges the gap..just wanted to know if it's been moved at all recently just so I'm not whistling in the wind literally..lol
rugerfanuk 2025-11-09 10:20 a.m.
Yup, Clee Hill East and South belong to me and are both a couple of miles from where I live. East was recently abducted, but has arisen again with added protection.
rugerfanuk
Yup, Clee Hill East and South belong to me and are both a couple of miles from where I live. East was recently abducted, but has arisen again with added protection.
likkleidiot_nwo 2025-11-09 10:25 a.m.
What stolen?...I'm setting up my mini Yagi to point at Clee Hill East..I get you occasionally which is ace as it's 81 mile away..so hopefully we get a more permanent connection
1
likkleidiot_nwo
What stolen?...I'm setting up my mini Yagi to point at Clee Hill East..I get you occasionally which is ace as it's 81 mile away..so hopefully we get a more permanent connection
rugerfanuk 2025-11-09 10:47 a.m.
Yup, was an easy target as it was solar. Now stealthy and running Li-SOCL2. YAGI should help a lot. Several other distant repeaters also point YAGI at it.
rugerfanuk
Yup, Clee Hill East and South belong to me and are both a couple of miles from where I live. East was recently abducted, but has arisen again with added protection.
Buzzard 2025-11-09 10:58 a.m.
What antennas do you use if you don't mind me asking?
rugerfanuk
Yup, was an easy target as it was solar. Now stealthy and running Li-SOCL2. YAGI should help a lot. Several other distant repeaters also point YAGI at it.
likkleidiot_nwo 2025-11-09 1:32 p.m.
What a great idea ..didn't know they could last that long..may look at converting some of my repeaters..what board or other hardware would I need if you don't mind me asking. Im currently running screenless T114 which seem very efficient but would like to learn more about these batteries..DM if you don't mind sharing a bit of setup info
Buzzard
What antennas do you use if you don't mind me asking?
rugerfanuk 2025-11-10 5:04 a.m.
Was using a 3dbi McGill and now they are both ALFA868.
1
likkleidiot_nwo
What a great idea ..didn't know they could last that long..may look at converting some of my repeaters..what board or other hardware would I need if you don't mind me asking. Im currently running screenless T114 which seem very efficient but would like to learn more about these batteries..DM if you don't mind sharing a bit of setup info
rugerfanuk 2025-11-10 5:13 a.m.
For repeaters, I have found the RAK boards to be the best for low power consumption with the T114 coming in a close second. Summer is Solar in a box with a couple of panels from Amazon ASIN - B0BDX4B6VX and winter is in a bit of 40mm pvc pipe with a couple of Li-SOCL2 from Amazzon too ASIN - B0BJZ6YN96.
1
rugerfanuk
For repeaters, I have found the RAK boards to be the best for low power consumption with the T114 coming in a close second. Summer is Solar in a box with a couple of panels from Amazon ASIN - B0BDX4B6VX and winter is in a bit of 40mm pvc pipe with a couple of Li-SOCL2 from Amazzon too ASIN - B0BJZ6YN96.
likkleidiot_nwo 2025-11-10 6:25 a.m.
Did you need to add a capacitor and a Schottky for the batteries?
likkleidiot_nwo
Did you need to add a capacitor and a Schottky for the batteries?
rugerfanuk 2025-11-10 6:31 a.m.
Just put a super capacitor in parallel and all seems to run well? Think that is only necessary for the more thirsty boards?
6:33 a.m.
New to this Li-SOCL2, but had been getting 60 days per 19Ah on MT which draws more current I think.
6:36 a.m.
The cold up here kills 18650s!
rugerfanuk
Just put a super capacitor in parallel and all seems to run well? Think that is only necessary for the more thirsty boards?

Buzzard 2025-11-10 2:31 p.m.
@Tyler - OlneyLoftBase I have wondered in the past with a meshtastic node whether or not it would be worthwhile asking the country parks if you could put one up with the offer of providing some metrics in exchange (the Pax counter was one I though about specifically). Buzzard

Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2025-11-10 6:35 p.m.
Could the mobile phone mast right next to 3shires cause issues with signal floor?
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase
Could the mobile phone mast right next to 3shires cause issues with signal floor?
Buzzard 2025-11-11 2:49 a.m.
Possibly/probably - there is band 20 at 800Mhz going up to 862Mhz and band 8 from 880Mhz upwards. It might be using neither one or both. (edited)
2:50 a.m.
A narrowerband antenna or a cavity filter might help
Buzzard
A narrowerband antenna or a cavity filter might help
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2025-11-11 9:03 a.m.
like this?
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase
like this?
Buzzard 2025-11-11 9:18 a.m.
Those usually have a maximum power of 20dbm or less so it might not be worth using them as you'd have less power emitted, plus 2-3db insertion loss. But might help with Rx. Something like these https://acasom.com/collections/helium could handle full output power, with less insertion loss but perhaps a bit big for a solar node. Could always try a different antenna - I use https://paradar.co.uk/products/868mhz-5dbi-indoor-whip for my node - I THINK the out of band noise rejection is pretty good. Hopefully with enough self-amalgamting tape and/or a cable gland it would stay dry. I get a noise floor of about -114 at home with it the whip, close to the town centre. When I get a chance I might head down close to 3Shires with my testing repeater and the paradar and see what figures I get
Helium 868M/915M
Explore ACASOM's manufacturing of signal jammers, amplifiers, signal boosters, and drone jammer products to enhance communication security in a premium way.
868Mhz 5dBi whip antenna with SMA + RP-SMA connector for LoRa, Mesh...
Free shipping via Royal Mail 48 to UK addresses The smallest of our range, our 5dBi indoor antennas are an enhanced tuned dipole design from brass tube, producing an extremely efficient response at the chosen frequency. The "old-fashioned" design is more efficient, less affected by nearby objects and interference and a
Buzzard
Those usually have a maximum power of 20dbm or less so it might not be worth using them as you'd have less power emitted, plus 2-3db insertion loss. But might help with Rx. Something like these https://acasom.com/collections/helium could handle full output power, with less insertion loss but perhaps a bit big for a solar node. Could always try a different antenna - I use https://paradar.co.uk/products/868mhz-5dbi-indoor-whip for my node - I THINK the out of band noise rejection is pretty good. Hopefully with enough self-amalgamting tape and/or a cable gland it would stay dry. I get a noise floor of about -114 at home with it the whip, close to the town centre. When I get a chance I might head down close to 3Shires with my testing repeater and the paradar and see what figures I get

Could the mobile phone mast right next to 3shires cause issues with signal floor?
Yano 2025-11-11 12:30 p.m.
Hey Mr T - what's wrong with the noise floor on 3 Shires? - looks healthy: -114dB
Yano
Hey Mr T - what's wrong with the noise floor on 3 Shires? - looks healthy: -114dB
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2025-11-11 12:31 p.m.
Buzzard thinks it might have an issue he seems to hear it pretty well but I dont think it hears him that well.
Yano 2025-11-11 1:45 p.m.
Well, if you trace around, there are a lot of asymmetric connections. 3 Shires consistently hears Alban Hill better than Alban Hill hears 3 Shires. But 3 Shires hears New Model better than New Model hears 3 Shires. And Alban Hill can hardly hear New Model, but New Model hears Alban Hill better. So, is New Model low on transmit? Or is 3 Shires low on receive? It's complex.
1:46 p.m.
I just did 10 consecutive traces between 3 Shires and CFD Cranfield (I find CFD is quite symmetric): Average is in red (edited)
1:47 p.m.
I would say it's fine..
Yano 2025-11-11 1:54 p.m.
Buzzard
@rugerfanuk What sorta noise floor are you getting at Clee Hill?
rugerfanuk 2025-11-11 1:54 p.m.
Clee Hill South-Rep is showing -100dB and East is -109dB.
1
Buzzard 2025-11-11 2:01 p.m.
I’m usually getting about -117,-118 at new model so that would perhaps explain the asymmetrical snrs on most traces I do.
2:03 p.m.
It was not for me an issue at 3Shires and more understandable why the asymmetry - equipment, location (noise floor) or both.
2:06 p.m.
In my testing at various points over the past few days New Model hears 3Shires about 3-4 db better than 3Shires hears Nee model. Generally New Model seems to hear better than it being heard.
Buzzard
I’m usually getting about -117,-118 at new model so that would perhaps explain the asymmetrical snrs on most traces I do.
rugerfanuk 2025-11-11 2:08 p.m.

Buzzard 2025-11-11 2:08 p.m.

Yano
I'm going for quick and dirty solution for the winter with a KSD-301 0°C NO on the solar output. UGLY!
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2025-11-11 4:21 p.m.
Is that to switch it off if its too cold?
Yano 2025-11-11 4:52 p.m.
Lithium batteries start to plate when they are charged below 0°C. So it remains on and will use the battery, but it will not charge when freezing.. (edited)
Yano 2025-11-11 4:59 p.m.
Not elegant, but simple. Like me..
Yano
Not elegant, but simple. Like me..
Buzzard 2025-11-11 5:01 p.m.
Well I've decided to shamelessly copy you and buy some of those for my next nodes - I wonder about perhpas retofitting new model with one....
Yano 2025-11-11 5:11 p.m.
It can't hurt. But.. I've just read a post in the Yorkshire forum https://discord.com/channels/1343693475589263471/1391912513737593033/1437887052212605071
5:13 p.m.
So maybe it's overkill
Buzzard 2025-11-11 5:18 p.m.
If that is true - probably - we aren't pupming that much current into the lipo's compared to say a mains/usb charger so perhaps it is less of an issue for our use case.
Yano 2025-11-11 5:33 p.m.
But those thermostatic switches don't use any current, so I'm going to put them in anyhow..

CAT 2025-11-12 5:15 a.m.

Steve ● 7acc 2025-11-12 5:30 a.m.

ninkosan 2025-11-12 7:15 a.m.
Thanks for info. Yeah outbound appears fine, can talk to/manage repeater fine… absolutely zero messages coming the other way to my client devices, even adverts
7:16 a.m.

Dayle - M0OUE 2025-11-12 7:35 a.m.
Have you checked all your cables? Similar things can happen when a cable breaks or an RP-SMA is used with an SMA antenna for example
Buzzard 2025-11-12 10:19 a.m.
Could be a difference in noise floor
Dayle - M0OUE
Have you checked all your cables? Similar things can happen when a cable breaks or an RP-SMA is used with an SMA antenna for example

ShadowDemon 2025-11-18 1:06 p.m.

ShadowDemon
Hi, this is one of mine that has gone flat, I have not been able to get to it due to a leg injury. I hope to get it rotated out in a couple of weeks.
ninkosan 2025-11-18 1:10 p.m.

ShadowDemon 2025-11-18 1:12 p.m.
They have been there since March and did quite well in the sun but winter has killed them all off. I will make this the first one I change if its helping the community.

rob.world 2025-11-22 4:23 p.m.
Ay up me ducks. Used Meshtastic quite a bit (haltec v3 and t-deck plus) and not used it for a while so thought I’d give Meshcore a go. I have a heltec node on my roof (still running meshtastic so will re-flash to mesh core once I can get neighbour help me with ladder). For time being I’m trying to reach someone (anyone!!) with the t-deck plus as to date on this attempt of using meshcore and previous attempts (a month ago and about 3 months ago) I’ve never made a single contact. Is there anyone in Derby city centre / Allestree area that is on Meshcore that fancies pinging me a message?
4:24 p.m.
I'm using EU/UK narrow and 869.617

Matt 2025-12-10 12:17 p.m.
Is that the overlap between Meshtastic and Meshcore? Signal on the Left being narrow (MC) and wider on the right (MT) or is it some of the 100’s of other things on the band, I’m seeing all sorts on the SDR
Matt
Is that the overlap between Meshtastic and Meshcore? Signal on the Left being narrow (MC) and wider on the right (MT) or is it some of the 100’s of other things on the band, I’m seeing all sorts on the SDR
CAT 2025-12-10 12:25 p.m.
The wide signal on the right is from a temporary traffic light that was put up, it is now gone. But I'm having lots of gas works done in my area. The one on the left that merges with the wide signal, not 100% what that is as it has been going for a while now.
12:26 p.m.
@Matt This is another traffic light that popped up but has also been moved. The blip is MC (edited)
CAT
The wide signal on the right is from a temporary traffic light that was put up, it is now gone. But I'm having lots of gas works done in my area. The one on the left that merges with the wide signal, not 100% what that is as it has been going for a while now.

Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2025-12-26 2:12 p.m.
Ive put up a repeater south facing in my window for now in lceiaster, just need a couple of bits to secure it to an outside pole. Its called UpTheFoxes LE3 let me know if anyone sees it.
bayjayzus 2025-12-28 9:44 p.m.
I'm in Telford, thinking about putting a repeater up but I seem to be bl00dy miles from anyone! #BlackHole
Steve ● 7acc 2025-12-29 4:49 a.m.
Stick something up the Wrekin, there's a MT node up there somewhere which is apparently working well
bayjayzus 2025-12-29 3:56 p.m.
There used to be a pirate radio station transmitting from the Wrekin years back so I think the authorities are quite twitchy about who is doing what up there, apparently they were even running a crafty power line from the TV broadcast building!. The other issue with the Wrekin is that Vodafone have recently been given planning permission for a 5G transmitter and there are also O2 and Three transmitters in the vicinity along with microwave and the elephant in the room a 64m digital TV mast, so there's lots of potential for interfence if you get it wrong. Looking at the Meshtastic map the Wrekin node appears to be on the NW of the Wrekin, quite a distance from & 20m or so lower than the peak, its targeting a node in Stafford. Looking at the Meshcore coverage tool there would be very little coverage to the South and East of the Wrekin (i.e me) without the use of several more repeaters to fill the gaps which appears to be what Meshtastic does in Telford where its coverage is actually pretty good, so it's nice to know it's doable. I'm doing some coverage tests as there are a few areas closer to me that have a c. 200m elevation but I still haven't found one with a decent line of sight to home there's old pit mounds everywhere around here getting in the way. I'm quite jealous of those in the S.E. they've got it relatively easy in comparison!.
bayjayzus 2025-12-29 4:33 p.m.
Oops I meant '200m or so lower than the peak', the transmitter appears to be positioned at half the height of the Wrekin assuming the map position is correct?
Steve ● 7acc 2025-12-29 5:02 p.m.
It's unlikely it's the exact position, but usually it'll be nearish-by based on other hill nodes

Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-01-03 8:42 a.m.

Steve ● 7acc

bayjayzus 2026-01-03 12:39 p.m.
So I went out with the dog on New Year's Day to have a nose for suitable locations to place my repeater. There's a pit mound just over the road that is about 155m amsl which is only 15m or so higher than my house but for the first time in a month I actually got responses on my T1000e and from as far away as Oldham to the Isle of Wight!.. So I'm guessing I was actually on the main mesh?. Weirdly though I was in Shrewsbury today, there's supposed to be a couple of Meshcore repeaters there but I got absolutely nothing, Shropshire really is a black hole for Meshcore atm.. anyway I'm now looking out for a suitably tall tree on that mound and somebody able-bodied enough to mount my repeater high up for me.. Watch this space!
bayjayzus
So I went out with the dog on New Year's Day to have a nose for suitable locations to place my repeater. There's a pit mound just over the road that is about 155m amsl which is only 15m or so higher than my house but for the first time in a month I actually got responses on my T1000e and from as far away as Oldham to the Isle of Wight!.. Watch this space!
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-03 1:15 p.m.
Definitely sounds like you got into the main mesh the west mids still has a lot of people using MT, they have made it clear they have no interest in MC so it's probably going to be quieter compared to elsewhere
bayjayzus
So I went out with the dog on New Year's Day to have a nose for suitable locations to place my repeater. Weirdly though I was in Shrewsbury today, there's supposed to be a couple of Meshcore repeaters there but I got absolutely nothing, Shropshire really is a black hole for Meshcore atm.. anyway I'm now looking out for a suitably tall tree on that mound and somebody able-bodied enough to mount my repeater high up for me.. Watch this space!
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-01-03 1:25 p.m.
100% that means you hit the main mesh
Shaun M7HSA 2026-01-03 1:25 p.m.
Hi all. Just stumbled accross this thread. Im based in Nuneaton and have a permanent repeater running in my loft using the heltec v4 and a diy yagi antenna pointing accross the town west towards Birmingham. It gives great coverage accross the town and using my portable node in can send messages back no problem. There are a couple of other repeaters in Nuneaton that are solar powered but i think they have gone offline recently as they only have small panels. Outside of Nuneaton we havent really picked much up unless out portable. There seems to be a bit of a gap between Coventry and Leicester and it would be great to see something good bridge the gap. Im also admin on the Facebook page UK midlands meshtastic and meschcore. Lots of new members popping up each day. Shaun M7HSA
Shaun M7HSA
Hi all. Just stumbled accross this thread. Im based in Nuneaton and have a permanent repeater running in my loft using the heltec v4 and a diy yagi antenna pointing accross the town west towards Birmingham. It gives great coverage accross the town and using my portable node in can send messages back no problem. There are a couple of other repeaters in Nuneaton that are solar powered but i think they have gone offline recently as they only have small panels. Outside of Nuneaton we havent really picked much up unless out portable. There seems to be a bit of a gap between Coventry and Leicester and it would be great to see something good bridge the gap. Im also admin on the Facebook page UK midlands meshtastic and meschcore. Lots of new members popping up each day. Shaun M7HSA

Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-01-04 11:27 a.m.

Tyler - OlneyLoftBase

Shaun M7HSA 2026-01-04 4:53 p.m.
Well tonight was a shock.A new local repeater came online in a great location which bridged the gap and managed to get us onto the mesh. 6 contacts in the list to over 100 and some good chatter going on in the public channel with contacts as far as the south coast.
4:53 p.m.
Game changer for our area.
Shaun M7HSA
Well tonight was a shock.A new local repeater came online in a great location which bridged the gap and managed to get us onto the mesh. 6 contacts in the list to over 100 and some good chatter going on in the public channel with contacts as far as the south coast.
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-01-04 4:55 p.m.
Ive said hello to you in public, which was teh repeater taht bridged you?
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase
Ive said hello to you in public, which was teh repeater taht bridged you?
Shaun M7HSA 2026-01-04 5:01 p.m.
Ah cool. The repeater is the Squire repeater which is the opposite of town in Ansley but has some good height up there. You can see accross the whole of Nuneaton and beyond over Leicestershire from there. Probably one of the highest locations in Warwickshire.
5:02 p.m.
My yagi points straight to that direction so my repeater is now getting in.
Shaun M7HSA
My yagi points straight to that direction so my repeater is now getting in.
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-01-04 5:03 p.m.

Shaun M7HSA 2026-01-04 5:10 p.m.

Rich 2026-01-08 7:34 a.m.
Unless you really need a magmount, i'd avoid these. I've tried a few and they rarely offer an improvement due to the loss in the coax. The links above are to decent antennas though.

James - M7MMD 2026-01-09 7:27 a.m.
Asking for a friend... What's the verdict on leaving repeaters in rural location? Probably aiming for public land as opposed to private property. I can't quite figure out the legality, do you label it something like Amateur Radio Project and put a phone number/email on it, omit contact details entirely and accept a potential loss, or do you leave it as a black box and hope somebody doesn't call the bomb squad?
James - M7MMD
Asking for a friend... What's the verdict on leaving repeaters in rural location? Probably aiming for public land as opposed to private property. I can't quite figure out the legality, do you label it something like Amateur Radio Project and put a phone number/email on it, omit contact details entirely and accept a potential loss, or do you leave it as a black box and hope somebody doesn't call the bomb squad?
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-01-09 8:01 a.m.
If its doing no harm and out the way and you actively maintain it, I dont see a huge problem. I suggest a birdhouse node, I literally picked mine up jsut now it had fallen over, its been up there in a public park for a couple of months.

I have 2 up on public land - one for about 3 months - all somewhat discreetly placed - so far so good.

I’ve added another 2 repeaters around Park Hall in Stoke on Trent this weekend. Brings the total to around 11 within the area. We seem to be connecting north now. I think MeshCore is the way if you want to relay messages over greater distances, MT fizzles out after 7 hops and can be frustrating.

botto 2026-01-19 5:35 a.m.
Is there a specific channel used for this part of England?
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-19 5:42 a.m.
There's #midlands but it's fairly quiet. There is still a lot of MT users around here who have no interest switching to MC
botto 2026-01-19 9:43 a.m.
yeah I've noticed, quite interesting considering the difference in range, so much further reach with MC
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-19 10:02 a.m.
I could give you my view as I've been around from almost the beginning but no point, they're happy doing their own thing, can't see it changing anytime soon.
botto
yeah I've noticed, quite interesting considering the difference in range, so much further reach with MC
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-01-19 12:10 p.m.
MC is limited by Line of site so the higher the better (curveture of the earth), but probably a lot of teh repeaters you see in the app are ones that you heard via the mesh rather than direct.
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase
MC is limited by Line of site so the higher the better (curveture of the earth), but probably a lot of teh repeaters you see in the app are ones that you heard via the mesh rather than direct.
Matt 2026-01-19 12:12 p.m.
So it isn’t flat then?
Matt
So it isn’t flat then?

Maybe the AI made it into a sphere?
12:16 p.m.
Seriously though, like they say height is might. I’ve watched from a far as many people try and up the power only to find they are just firing more RF into the side of the hill with no positive outcome.
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase
MC is limited by Line of site so the higher the better (curveture of the earth), but probably a lot of teh repeaters you see in the app are ones that you heard via the mesh rather than direct.
botto 2026-01-19 1:10 p.m.
Yeah, I meant reach in regards to number of hops you get with MC But yes it’s mostly line of sight, I guess you can get a little bit from reflection
1:14 p.m.
Not sure about protocol differences, it’s all lora but when I used to run nodes in bham I got almost no traffic (granted more than 2 years ago now)
Matt 2026-01-19 1:21 p.m.
Meshtastic is a Mesh, MeshCore is an infrastructure Both have their merits and drawbacks. If it’s distance you want to achieve MC wins hands down. If it’s a post apocalyptic grid down scenario, where nodes randomly appear and drop out, MT is probably the best option. All down to your use case, but unless you have a spare module in an EMP proof case, if SHTF it’s all pretty useless if that scenario ever occurs.
botto 2026-01-19 1:25 p.m.
Module+Phone/PC+Power
1
1:25 p.m.
Also assuming everyone else does that
1:26 p.m.
I'd love to go past just using MeshCore and set up microwave links (i.e. what some people did in New York to build a ip mesh) but I know it would require a stupid amount of relays
Matt 2026-01-19 1:27 p.m.
It’s nice that MeshCore is picking up round here. I know I’ve said it umpteen times, it’s a shame there’s a frequency overlap or both systems would run nicely side by side without issue. If Meshtastic ran the same narrow bandwidth as MeshCore that might work, but not sure how that would play out with the amount of traffic it generates. MC is lean in comparison to MT.
botto 2026-01-19 1:28 p.m.
Yeah, not thought about that, I'll see if I can get my SDR set up, curious to see if there is a lot of noise from both system running
1:28 p.m.
In theory you could run MT on 433 (although all the equipment change over would be awful)
1:29 p.m.
then again, lots of security systems use 433
Matt 2026-01-19 1:36 p.m.
Could move MC to one of the other ISM frequencies close enough to the existing so no extra hardware needed, MT doesn’t fit the profile as every node is a client, whereas with MC the infrastructure is different.
botto
In theory you could run MT on 433 (although all the equipment change over would be awful)
Matt 2026-01-19 1:38 p.m.
433 is limited to 10mw unless you have a ham radio licence and even then you lose the encryption to comply and also would have to stick to the top part of the band and you have the same issue as LoRa APRS and MeshCom are up there on the same frequency
1:40 p.m.
439.9125 and 125kHz bandwidth seems to be the settings used on that band by licensed Hams Quite a bit of activity around Market Harborough
merko 2026-01-19 5:21 p.m.
Hi, I'm new to this. I installed meshos on a lilygo T-deck and have wondered around Brum all day but not received a single thing Am I missing some vital settings or something???
merko
Hi, I'm new to this. I installed meshos on a lilygo T-deck and have wondered around Brum all day but not received a single thing Am I missing some vital settings or something???
James - M7MMD 2026-01-19 5:23 p.m.
The obvious one (when you know) is to make sure you have set radio settings to UK Narrow.
James - M7MMD
The obvious one (when you know) is to make sure you have set radio settings to UK Narrow.
merko 2026-01-19 5:24 p.m.
Yes, I set it to that on installation and haven't picked up a single anything all day
merko
Yes, I set it to that on installation and haven't picked up a single anything all day
merko 2026-01-19 5:27 p.m.
My system info says: 869.62MHz SF8 BW62 CR4/8 TX22
James - M7MMD 2026-01-19 5:28 p.m.
Looks about right! Are you near any repeaters on the internet map. Might be worth doing a scan when you are near those.
Matt 2026-01-19 5:31 p.m.
It all seems to be happening on Meshtastic over that way, a few of them starting to dabble though so I don’t think it’ll be long
James - M7MMD
Looks about right! Are you near any repeaters on the internet map. Might be worth doing a scan when you are near those.

Matt 2026-01-19 5:41 p.m.

Matt
Says updated 1 month ago, I know a few of the MeshTastic lot tried it and switched back because there wasn’t much happening
merko 2026-01-19 5:44 p.m.
I have got a meshtastic node aswell, that literally picks up hundreds of node's. I'm just a bit shocked that meshcore has picked up nothing all day I have been to city centre, Aston/lozells, nechells, small heath and Yardley n not a single signal. That's why I'm wondering if it's a missing setting?
Matt 2026-01-19 5:45 p.m.
Hall Green isn’t going to happen direct from me
merko
I have got a meshtastic node aswell, that literally picks up hundreds of node's. I'm just a bit shocked that meshcore has picked up nothing all day I have been to city centre, Aston/lozells, nechells, small heath and Yardley n not a single signal. That's why I'm wondering if it's a missing setting?
Matt 2026-01-19 5:50 p.m.
I tried it at the end of last year, only thing I could hear was Clee Hill, and couldn’t get back to it, so switched to Meshtastic, I have a solid link to Nuneaton now, so just need to move the antenna and set the node up properly. I’m going to do some site tests next week, don’t think I’ll get it done before then but it’d be nice if I could help with another hop.
5:52 p.m.
Is there much happening out your way @James - M7MMD ?
Matt
Is there much happening out your way @James - M7MMD ?
James - M7MMD 2026-01-20 2:31 a.m.
Only managed to get one contact and a few repeaters which I think are run by that one person, but I'm not giving up. I guess many people try once, get nothing and give up. Which is why I think/hope if I can get a strategically placed repeater then those people will try, succeed and stay. And, erm, hopefully it'll explode from there.
MadAde 2026-01-20 5:46 a.m.
Can report significantly more activity in and around the Derby area
3
James - M7MMD
Only managed to get one contact and a few repeaters which I think are run by that one person, but I'm not giving up. I guess many people try once, get nothing and give up. Which is why I think/hope if I can get a strategically placed repeater then those people will try, succeed and stay. And, erm, hopefully it'll explode from there.
Matt 2026-01-20 9:10 a.m.
I think a lot of that happens, I talk to people on Meshtastic and they say they tried MeshCore but no nodes around them so they stuck to Meshtastic, ironically if they didn’t switch back they would have eventually have had a half decent mesh. I’m on the edge of a pretty decent MT mesh and they don’t seem to want to switch, so I’m hoping I can get both to work with minimum interaction. (edited)

Shaun M7HSA 2026-01-20 11:26 a.m.
How's it all going. Meshcore working pretty well over here in Nuneaton at the min. Think 3 have 3 repeaters now covering the town and beyond with the new squire one putting the rest of us on the map as he is high up. More to come online shortly I hope but making contacts frequently all the way down to the isle of white. You guys in Leicestershire should be connected soon. Had one lad get through with just a rubber duck antenna from Bosworth direction.

6:39 p.m.

princess 2026-01-22 3:43 a.m.
from what i tested in bham, there is only my repeater in Selly Oak(hears Clee Hill), and another in Longbridge, that cover useful area. The latter doesn’t seem to link to the wider mesh, but got good ears. I currently have temp diy hw, that is old esp32 and ebyte 1w module, but those are quite deaf on rx. The storm last week had too much static and fried both my heltec v4s. Getting with a compromised rubber duck antenna is possible, heltec v4 , outside on the window sill, second floor in Droiwitch, i have stable link to Clee Hill South. But repeater needs to have ears. I have tried meshtastic, seems to have an easy link at home, but the app is so confusing, you can’t tell what node is RF, what is internet. Saying hello on the public channel - nothing. Meshcore has a bit of a charm and mistery, like making an impossible wspr contact to europe on 4m in the summer.
1
princess
from what i tested in bham, there is only my repeater in Selly Oak(hears Clee Hill), and another in Longbridge, that cover useful area. The latter doesn’t seem to link to the wider mesh, but got good ears. I currently have temp diy hw, that is old esp32 and ebyte 1w module, but those are quite deaf on rx. The storm last week had too much static and fried both my heltec v4s. Getting with a compromised rubber duck antenna is possible, heltec v4 , outside on the window sill, second floor in Droiwitch, i have stable link to Clee Hill South. But repeater needs to have ears. I have tried meshtastic, seems to have an easy link at home, but the app is so confusing, you can’t tell what node is RF, what is internet. Saying hello on the public channel - nothing. Meshcore has a bit of a charm and mistery, like making an impossible wspr contact to europe on 4m in the summer.

S7uart G5AIL 2026-01-23 6:24 p.m.
Pushed a few new bits to meshrank.net tonight: • Personalised channels • You can now add channels and see what’s popular • More progress on Repeater Rank • Added low-scoring neighbours (the ones the app effectively ignores) — handy for seeing how far your signals are actually travelling • Next up: more work on live mesh stats

Yeah it doesn't seem to honour the
Steve ● 7acc
How do people opt out of being ranked? I run a feed to the meshcore map and looking at the log messages I get back there are several repeaters which are suppressed from public view. I see nothing similar with your site.
S7uart G5AIL 2026-01-26 1:52 p.m.

S7uart G5AIL
I don’t include repeaters with implausible GPS (edited)
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-26 1:55 p.m.

S7uart G5AIL
I don’t include repeaters with implausible GPS (edited)

Ive seen some on there with the in there name which are ones that dont want to be on the map.
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase
Ive seen some on there with the in there name which are ones that dont want to be on the map.
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-26 1:59 p.m.

S7uart G5AIL 2026-01-26 2:00 p.m.
I’m only displaying adverts that are being openly transmitted accross the mesh. If someone wants to remain anonymous I’m more than happy to discuss. Just send me an email to Stu.stoke@gmail.com
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-26 2:03 p.m.
There shouldn't need to be discussion, just do the same thing as all the other sites are
S7uart G5AIL 2026-01-26 2:12 p.m.
I wasn’t aware of the emoji relevance. If you can send me the relevant info regarding this I’ll sort it out
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-26 2:26 p.m.
This is one taken from your site
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-01-26 2:30 p.m.
Its not actually documented
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase
Its not actually documented
Matt 2026-01-26 2:33 p.m.
I was about to say I can’t see it in the official documentation. Nor does the mesh data carry a flag for this. Other than that emoji. Maybe it needs to be added as a request for it to be added to the documentation so developers know not to break a little known and unwritten rule? (edited)
Steve ● 7acc
There shouldn't need to be discussion, just do the same thing as all the other sites are
Matt 2026-01-26 2:44 p.m.
It was actually a great idea as there is no flag in the packets to inform others. Did it originate from a community forum? Might use it on some of my clients.
Matt
It was actually a great idea as there is no flag in the packets to inform others. Did it originate from a community forum? Might use it on some of my clients.
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-26 2:47 p.m.
Tbh I don't know, I only started noticing it once I started the map feed and was checking for errors, as I think @recrof runs the map maybe he can tell us how the came into existence
Matt 2026-01-26 2:49 p.m.
It’s all quite interesting @Steve ● 7acc , pretty sure @S7uart G5AIL didn’t intentionally upload data that people wanted opting out, just unaware of it as was I
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-26 2:50 p.m.
It's certainly worth considering, while I don't have any issues with meshrank the wardriving one is a bit more spooky, but they say they respect the emoji
Steve ● 7acc

Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-26 2:51 p.m.

recrof 2026-01-26 2:52 p.m.
somebody from UK wanted to remove their nodes from internet map for good, that was moment I added no entry emoji check
recrof
somebody from UK wanted to remove their nodes from internet map for good, that was moment I added no entry emoji check
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-26 2:53 p.m.
Should have guessed it would be someone from here, thanks for answering
Matt 2026-01-26 2:58 p.m.
Beats me why people that have such privacy concerns post a geolocation in the 1st place. I get that someone may have posted it in the past and later regretted it, but why carry on posting it and saying here is my location, please don’t use it? Is it just me that finds that odd?
Steve ● 7acc 2026-01-26 3:02 p.m.
Think some of the preppers among us probably think it gives them some tactical advantage
Steve ● 7acc
Think some of the preppers among us probably think it gives them some tactical advantage
Matt 2026-01-26 3:05 p.m.
I would say the opposite, share it with your own team, but in a tactical scenario, you wouldn’t broadcast your co-ordinates to the enemy? Although there was a case, where a terrorist uploaded geotagged media to the internet and was taken out by a drone strike, (so it was reported)
S7uart G5AIL 2026-01-26 3:06 p.m.

It’s down at the moment, going back up on the mast with one of these tomorrow to see how it performs. https://www.mcgillmicrowave.com/mcgill-optimised-9-dbi-868/?attachment_id=24683&download_file=xvryrneiu1n4o
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-02-02 1:48 p.m.
Nice heard good things about Mcgill
Matt 2026-02-02 1:49 p.m.
Still won’t outperform a carefully positioned rubber duck on a hill though.
1
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase
Nice heard good things about Mcgill
Matt 2026-02-02 1:49 p.m.
It’s good but it’s bloody heavy for what it is.
Matt 2026-02-02 2:00 p.m.
I’ll see how it goes, it might end up back on Meshtastic and a Yagi on MeshCore. I didn’t think the 2 would play nicely with each other having a slight overlap in Frequency, but they seem OK. Just don’t want them too close to each other in case it damages the front end. Might dial the RX gain back a bit on the MT, the MC is already a bit deaf on a Heltec V4 unless it’s patched. I’m waiting for a delivery of some new boards to replace one of these V4 modules. Got a box of Seeed nRF kits to build when I get a chance, but been really busy and not had much spare time unfortunately. I have a solar node on the shelf that was going to be installed in Desford, but trying to find a better location.
Matt
I’ll see how it goes, it might end up back on Meshtastic and a Yagi on MeshCore. I didn’t think the 2 would play nicely with each other having a slight overlap in Frequency, but they seem OK. Just don’t want them too close to each other in case it damages the front end. Might dial the RX gain back a bit on the MT, the MC is already a bit deaf on a Heltec V4 unless it’s patched. I’m waiting for a delivery of some new boards to replace one of these V4 modules. Got a box of Seeed nRF kits to build when I get a chance, but been really busy and not had much spare time unfortunately. I have a solar node on the shelf that was going to be installed in Desford, but trying to find a better location.

S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-04 2:20 p.m.
What device are you using for your repeater?
S7uart G5AIL
What device are you using for your repeater?
Stu 2026-02-04 2:24 p.m.
Sensecap solar P1 with this antenna https://paradar.co.uk/products/868mhz-hnt-lora-antenna-long-range-4-5dbi
868Mhz tuned 4.5dBi antenna for HNT and LoRa
Extend the range of your Helium hotspot with our tuned LoRa / HNT antenna, for outdoor use. Designed in the United Kingdom from aviation-grade components, and provides excellent range for HNT (Helium) hotspots as well as for aviation (FLARM or OGN) applications. Compatible antenna for all Helium miners - Bobcat, RAK, and Nebra miner antenna.

Matt 2026-02-04 4:28 p.m.

S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-04 4:39 p.m.
Matt, if you send me an email to Stu.stoke@gmail.com with your address I’ll get a heltec v3 sent out in the post. It will connect to MeshRank.net as an observer. Once it’s setup you will start to see what’s going on. The site can see partially decoded packets so it may help to place your antenna.
4:39 p.m.
Just this alone will help you.
4:41 p.m.
If you don’t mind keeping it running then you can keep it. It will also help other users who may put repeaters up locally
S7uart G5AIL
Matt, if you send me an email to Stu.stoke@gmail.com with your address I’ll get a heltec v3 sent out in the post. It will connect to MeshRank.net as an observer. Once it’s setup you will start to see what’s going on. The site can see partially decoded packets so it may help to place your antenna.

Matt
Seems to me that MC is more national and MT communities are small pockets like tribes that don’t get along
S7uart G5AIL 2026-02-05 2:35 p.m.
I think of MeshCORE as HF and Meshtastic as VHF Both great just different. Everyone should have both nodes tbh
1
Matt 2026-02-05 2:40 p.m.
MC is more like an infrastructure than a Mesh I like how MT clients repeat messages, also like how MC generates less traffic. Can’t have it both ways I guess. Each has strengths and weaknesses. Like I always say, it’s a shame there’s an overlap in frequency. The 2 do work side by side, but it’s not ideal.
2:44 p.m.
MC seems lightening fast in comparison to MT and not being capped to 7 hops is great. If you are localised MT is OK Even 1200baud AX25 in the 90’d seemed to get more distance than MT though.
Matt 2026-02-05 2:54 p.m.
That would solve the 7 hop issue (overthinking again)

Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-02-14 1:16 p.m.
You dont need a neighbour to get out as it routes via flood, whcih means you have good RX but bad TX, so you need a reeapter nearby to get you out the bowl

Thought so.

mikeyp 2026-02-15 3:31 a.m.
Morning all. Ok. I can now talk with @redmozzy ok. He could do with his antenna being external but that's ok. I think my repeater is ok. I think, having slept on it, there are a couple of factors at play here. We both live in a bowl. There is relatively little noise and interference and having a high gain antenna, we're able to hear things broadcast from a way away, hence picking up Bardon Hill. That said, Bardon Hill is high up, noisier and may not be able to hear my repeater over everything else. Also, I'm so low to sea level where I am, that despite elevating my repeater as much as I possibly can, I still don't quite have line of sight of the Bardon Hill repeater.
mikeyp
Morning all. Ok. I can now talk with @redmozzy ok. He could do with his antenna being external but that's ok. I think my repeater is ok. I think, having slept on it, there are a couple of factors at play here. We both live in a bowl. There is relatively little noise and interference and having a high gain antenna, we're able to hear things broadcast from a way away, hence picking up Bardon Hill. That said, Bardon Hill is high up, noisier and may not be able to hear my repeater over everything else. Also, I'm so low to sea level where I am, that despite elevating my repeater as much as I possibly can, I still don't quite have line of sight of the Bardon Hill repeater.
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-02-15 9:35 a.m.
So I logged in an noise floor on it isnt great its at -95
9:35 a.m.
which is not great
mikeyp 2026-02-15 10:07 a.m.
So I need to shout louder?
10:11 a.m.
Tbh that’s not massively surprising given it’s on a hill with masses of visibility of anything on the lora frequencies. Don’t get me wrong, it’s extremely valuable up there.
mikeyp
Tbh that’s not massively surprising given it’s on a hill with masses of visibility of anything on the lora frequencies. Don’t get me wrong, it’s extremely valuable up there.
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-02-15 10:26 a.m.
Barden hill has a huge tower for nation grid with a dozen Antennas on it
10:26 a.m.
im guessing its getting too much noise so it needs a cavity filter
10:27 a.m.
@Buzzard has offered to come up with me to install it and move it to a tower nearby (dont want to do it on my own for safety reasons).
mikeyp 2026-02-15 10:27 a.m.
For sure. Sounds good.
10:27 a.m.
Will message you for thoughts on my idea.
mikeyp
Will message you for thoughts on my idea.
Tyler - OlneyLoftBase 2026-02-15 10:28 a.m.
take a companion to the spot you want and check teh noise floor via tools
10:28 a.m.
-95 and you will end up like Barden hill but -120 is amazing

mikeyp 2026-02-15 6:18 p.m.
I’m in a geographical bowl with little ability to get a signal to the outside world. Sucks really. Trying to work out a good place to get a repeater up that will get me “out”.

S7uart G5AIL 2026-03-06 3:15 a.m.
Hi chaps. Just thought I’d introduce myself. I’m a Uk ham, I’ve tried to build some free tools that you can use to help establish or strengthen your mesh. The site is called Meshrank.net. I’m actually looking for someone who would like to act as an observer in this area. If you setup an Observer node you also get access to some private functions like a Live map. Hope it’s of interest to You. It’s very much work in progress. MeshRank.net
S7uart G5AIL
Hi chaps. Just thought I’d introduce myself. I’m a Uk ham, I’ve tried to build some free tools that you can use to help establish or strengthen your mesh. The site is called Meshrank.net. I’m actually looking for someone who would like to act as an observer in this area. If you setup an Observer node you also get access to some private functions like a Live map. Hope it’s of interest to You. It’s very much work in progress. MeshRank.net
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-06 8:00 a.m.

S7uart G5AIL
Hi chaps. Just thought I’d introduce myself. I’m a Uk ham, I’ve tried to build some free tools that you can use to help establish or strengthen your mesh. The site is called Meshrank.net. I’m actually looking for someone who would like to act as an observer in this area. If you setup an Observer node you also get access to some private functions like a Live map. Hope it’s of interest to You. It’s very much work in progress. MeshRank.net

Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-08 10:51 p.m.
Starting to think Derby has some trouble getting packets out of the area. Most of the time messages I send only make it around the local repeaters. I can receive messages. Not all of them though. Sending out maybe 1 in 10 get picked up by other nodes and broadcasted around. You can see what I get on here https://analyzer.letsmesh.net/packets?repeater_a=68DE80236AC51F37FA397DC3FD822A36A3661D99C6E68DBB71AE41BAE9410A5F Any one else from Derby seeing the same thing?

S7uart G5AIL 2026-03-09 3:14 a.m.
Try using MeshRank.net it has the greatest coverage of UKobservers. You get a more accurate result (edited)
3:16 a.m.
Also check your sending node is advertising its location. Without that data it limits the ability to Estimate the starting point so it shows less routes (edited)

ninkosan

Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-09 12:28 p.m.
I thought BT took over from Airwave?
ninkosan 2026-03-09 12:29 p.m.
Nah it’s its own thing still. EE will be doing the radio bit of the replacement if it ever arrives
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-09 12:30 p.m.
Ah. Someone told me a fib then. They said that BT were taking over all their stuff and moving it their 4G network. Guess it is just the 4G bit then. Wonder if Airwave will dump that site in a couple years then...
ninkosan 2026-03-09 12:32 p.m.
Tbh I can’t remember the exact extent to which BT are doing which bits of now. It might be most of it these days but it’s been a while since I looked
12:32 p.m.
It’s just been delayed for years and years, so they’re still distinct for now
Rich 2026-03-09 12:33 p.m.
Pretty sure the network is owned by Motorola now isnt it?
ninkosan 2026-03-09 12:33 p.m.
Current airwave is yep. ESN (replacement) was originally mostly Motorola too until someone decided that might be a competition-related problem too
12:34 p.m.
You’d think that might be obvious, but…
ninkosan
Current airwave is yep. ESN (replacement) was originally mostly Motorola too until someone decided that might be a competition-related problem too
Rich 2026-03-09 12:35 p.m.
Motorola lost out on part of ESN and then told the government that the current TETRA system needed a massive update or it would stop working!
ninkosan 2026-03-09 12:37 p.m.
You could argue there wasn’t much incentive to do the new one at speed when you’re making £££ off extending the life of the old one. But then I’m cynical like that
Rich 2026-03-09 12:38 p.m.
Exactly! Plus they've made billions off it in the process
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-09 12:39 p.m.
Its the British way
12:40 p.m.
I kid you not. When we get gov work it at PCBA, we are allowed to just quote it and add a digit to the end.
Rich 2026-03-09 12:40 p.m.
Yep crazy stuff but its true

Buzzard 2026-03-14 6:31 a.m.
E22 or e22p?
1
Buzzard
E22 or e22p?
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-14 7:50 a.m.
E22. I have an E22p on its way. (edited)
Jack @ PCBA.UK
E22. I have an E22p on its way. (edited)
Buzzard 2026-03-14 9:03 a.m.
Yeah the rx on the E22, like the heltec v4 is amplified but unfiltered and so the performance can be quite bad. I've found the e22p receive quite impressive in my own testing - comparable and perhaps better than my home's mashup of an amp and filter.
9:03 a.m.
and better than a plain sx1262 on a tbeam supreme
Buzzard
and better than a plain sx1262 on a tbeam supreme
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-14 9:13 a.m.
If it is better than stock SX1262 then I am happy. Right now I can't hear nodes 10-15km away but they can hear me. Annoying
Jack @ PCBA.UK
If it is better than stock SX1262 then I am happy. Right now I can't hear nodes 10-15km away but they can hear me. Annoying
Buzzard 2026-03-14 9:14 a.m.
Do you know what your nosie floor is?
Buzzard

Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-14 9:19 a.m.

Jack @ PCBA.UK
Take it for what it is. This is from the calculated output of the E22
Buzzard 2026-03-14 9:20 a.m.
The e22 figure will be high due to the lna - do you know what it is just using a plain sx1262?
Buzzard
The e22 figure will be high due to the lna - do you know what it is just using a plain sx1262?
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-14 9:51 a.m.
9:51 a.m.
I get that the LNA will raise the floor but the actual RX performance is worse as well
Jack @ PCBA.UK
Click to see attachment 🖼️
Buzzard 2026-03-14 9:53 a.m.
-110 isn't that bad but it isn't brilliant - the spike in the middle is concerning unless that is just picking up meshcore packets. In my experience and from what i've read - LNA's without a filter tend to have poor performance.
Buzzard
-110 isn't that bad but it isn't brilliant - the spike in the middle is concerning unless that is just picking up meshcore packets. In my experience and from what i've read - LNA's without a filter tend to have poor performance.
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-14 9:53 a.m.
It is just a mesh packet. Picking from screenshots I have on hand
Jack @ PCBA.UK

Buzzard 2026-03-14 9:54 a.m.
A filtered receiver, whether using an e22p or using an external filter might help you. (edited)
Buzzard
A filtered receiver, whether using an e22p or using an external filter might help you. (edited)
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-14 9:55 a.m.
I tried a filter and even left it open with the TX off, same noise floor.
9:56 a.m.
Not sure leaving it open would show anything.. Not into the dark arts (edited)
Buzzard 2026-03-14 9:56 a.m.
If the noise is in-band then a filter won't help.
9:58 a.m.
If you are using a tx amp and the nodes that can hear you but you can't hear are not, that would also explain it.
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-14 10:18 a.m.
I only tested the filter to see if the internal noise floor would drop. It didn't. Unless if i did it wrong
Buzzard 2026-03-14 10:20 a.m.
If the interference is intermittent - then it might be that it wasn't happening when the filter was connected but I would expect a filter to lower the noise floor - especially if your noise floor is around -110 dbm. What filter did you use btw?
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-14 10:24 a.m.
Paradar 868MHz
Buzzard 2026-03-14 10:26 a.m.
did you transmit though it? It is rated as only supporting 13dbm - so a plain sx1262 might fry it, an e22 even more likely.
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-14 10:26 a.m.
TX disabled
Buzzard 2026-03-14 10:26 a.m.
Ahh k

andykirby 2026-03-17 8:33 a.m.
==== Quick message about Bots please ==== There's an increasing number of bots appearing on the mesh in the UK, and I presume its the same everywhere! Whilst its fun to experiment with this stuff it causes a huge amount of unnecessary traffic especially when there's 5 bots replying to the same test message! We have to remember that in most regions we have one RF channel! Thanks for your consideration! Long live the Mesh!
1
andykirby
==== Quick message about Bots please ==== There's an increasing number of bots appearing on the mesh in the UK, and I presume its the same everywhere! Whilst its fun to experiment with this stuff it causes a huge amount of unnecessary traffic especially when there's 5 bots replying to the same test message! We have to remember that in most regions we have one RF channel! Thanks for your consideration! Long live the Mesh!

MeshCore Analyzer - LetsMesh.net
Real-time MeshCore mesh network analytics, node tracking, packet analysis, and coverage maps.
shaunokeefe
Are these the best settings ? I feel like I’m missing so much of the chat. If I goto the web version https://analyzer.letsmesh.net/channels and search for United Kingdom I’m missing so much
Jack @ PCBA.UK 2026-03-20 1:35 p.m.
you wont get the whole mesh as there are limits such as how many hops between the sender and you there are which has recently halved. Go to the Tools menu and use the "Discover Nearby Nodes" to see how many repeaters, if any, you are connected to.
1:36 p.m.
Derby did have some issues getting connected out of the area but repeater (DMC) SFE_R01 has been adjusted the other day and we now get solid connectivity to Litchfield and Nottingham. I am trying to add a node out towards Ashbourne to improve connection to Stoke.

bayjayzus 2026-04-01 6:47 a.m.
I've been trying to run my Pig on the Wall repeater on multibyte as apparently I have a (very weak) mutual link to the main mesh through Clee Hill East which is also multibyte. It's not going well TBH I've barely been able to get any messages out in days Meshrank is showing no message activity from me either) I'm seriously thinking about reverting back. Maybe its early days for multibyte, it will probably work better when 1.15 is released? (edited)

Mart 2026-04-21 10:11 a.m.
Nice job. And I've just received an advert from your companion although I'm not sure how given the topography between us. And I may have received a message via your repeater some time last week via the Mapperley repeater when it was last online.

bunnios002 2026-04-21 11:42 a.m.
Yeah, it's been up and down for testing as I wanted to make sure the batteries could hold charge and now it's in its final position.. I've been speaking to a chap who has the Westdale Rak Repeater and doing some testing but it's so hit and miss. He could ping mine every now and again but I couldn't get out to reply. I've just been in Arnold with the companion as I wanted to stop and see where I could ping my own repeater. Basically it stops around Gunthorpe so i might need to speak to a few people in between to see if they'd allow me to place a repeater on their property.
Mart 2026-04-21 11:49 a.m.
Similar here. I'm receiving from South Notts albeit with a lot of errors and my antenna is in the loft. Unfortunately, not possible for me to tx via the same route. I did better with the Mapperley top repeater which should reach you too when it is online
